commit.message: Bump cua-agent to v0.7.33

feat(agent): Add GPT 5.4 support for Computer tool (#1150)

* feat(agent): Add GPT 5.4 support for Computer tool via function calling

- Update OpenAI loop regex to match gpt-5.4 models
- Fix tool schema format for Responses API (parameters at root level)
- Add screenshot action handler to BrowserTool
- Handle function_call items for computer tool in agent.py
- Return screenshots as user messages with input_image content
  (function_call_output only supports text, so images must be sent
  as separate user messages for the model to see them)
- Add on_function_call_end callback for trajectory screenshots
- Filter action arguments to only pass relevant params per action type

Usage example:
```python
from agent import ComputerAgent
from computer import Computer

computer = Computer(provider_type="cloud", name="vm-name")
agent = ComputerAgent(
    tools=[computer],
    model="openai/gpt-5.4",
    api_key="your-openai-api-key",
)

async for result in agent.run([{"role": "user", "content": "Open terminal and run ls"}]):
    print(result)
```

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(agent): Address code review feedback for GPT 5.4 support

- Return actual action_result in function_call_output instead of hardcoded string
- Fix zero filtering in action_args (allow valid x=0, y=0 coordinates)
- Handle sibling user messages with screenshots in trajectory_saver
- Add terminate action and right_click method to cuaComputerHandler
- Update drag() to support both path and coordinate-based formats
- Fix predict_click() to use correct tool format for non-native models

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Switch docs agent from Anthropic to AWS Bedrock with Vercel OIDC (#1143)

* feat(docs): switch docs agent from Anthropic API to Bedrock with OIDC

Replace direct Anthropic API usage with AWS Bedrock via Vercel OIDC
authentication, matching the pattern used by cuabot. This eliminates
the need for an ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in production.

- Add @ai-sdk/amazon-bedrock and @vercel/functions dependencies
- Create Bedrock provider with OIDC credential flow and caching
- Pass LanguageModel instance to BuiltInAgent instead of string
- Support static AWS credentials fallback for local development
- Update .env.example with AWS_ROLE_ARN and credential vars

https://claude.ai/code/session_01CFThvyGiJzdFbKYsyUGMp3

* fix(docs): add @aws-sdk/credential-provider-web-identity for OIDC

The @vercel/functions/oidc module requires this package at runtime to
exchange Vercel OIDC tokens for AWS credentials via
sts:AssumeRoleWithWebIdentity. It's listed as an optional peer dep
of @vercel/functions but needs to be explicitly installed.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01CFThvyGiJzdFbKYsyUGMp3

* Update docs/src/app/api/copilotkit/route.ts

Co-authored-by: coderabbitai[bot] <136622811+coderabbitai[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(docs): migrate prompt-analyzer from Anthropic SDK to Bedrock OIDC

The cron prompt-digest was silently returning empty results because
prompt-analyzer.ts depended on ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, which isn't set in
the Vercel deployment (we use Bedrock via OIDC now).

- Replace @anthropic-ai/sdk with @ai-sdk/amazon-bedrock + ai SDK
  generateText(), using the same OIDC credential flow as copilotkit
- Remove unused @ai-sdk/anthropic and @anthropic-ai/sdk dependencies
- Add ai (Vercel AI SDK v6) as explicit dependency

https://claude.ai/code/session_01CFThvyGiJzdFbKYsyUGMp3

* fix(docs): remove stray closing brace in copilotkit route

Extra `}` at line 466 caused a parse error during Vercel build.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01CFThvyGiJzdFbKYsyUGMp3

fix(cua-bench): add KeyAction support to worker client (#1088) (#1141)

The worker client only accepted HotkeyAction for key presses, but the
Slack oracle uses KeyAction(key="Return"). Add `key` to valid_fn_names
and handle it in check_and_fix_action so callers can express single-key
presses matching the oracle behavior.

Closes #1088

commit.message:
fix(keyboard): normalize command key to cmd for pynput compatibility on macOS (#1135)

* fix(keyboard): normalize 'command' key to 'cmd' for pynput compatibility on macOS

The client Key.COMMAND enum has value 'command', but pynput's Key enum
uses 'cmd'. This mismatch causes all macOS Command shortcuts (cmd+a,
cmd+c, cmd+v, etc.) to silently fail with 'Unknown key: command'.

Fix both sides for defense in depth:
- Client (generic.py): normalize 'command' -> 'cmd' before sending
- Backend (macos.py): resolve 'command' -> 'cmd' on receipt, with
  warning/error logs on unknown keys and exceptions

* fix(keyboard): apply command alias resolution to key_down/key_up on macOS

* refactor

commit.message:
feat(agent): add telemetry to TypeScript agent SDK (#852)

Add PostHog telemetry to the @trycua/agent package for parity with the
Python SDK. This enables tracking of SDK usage, agent requests, and
errors.

Events added:
- module_init: Emitted when package is imported
- agent_client_initialized: Emitted when AgentClient is created
- agent_request_start: Emitted when responses.create() is called
- agent_request_end: Emitted on successful response
- agent_request_error: Emitted on request failure

Properties tracked include session_id, request_id, model, duration,
token usage, and error classification.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(lume): fix pause() and resume() incorrectly calling start() (#1130)

* fix(lume): fix pause() and resume() calling start() instead of correct VZVirtualMachine methods

Both `pause()` and `resume()` in `BaseVirtualizationService` were
incorrectly calling `virtualMachine.start` instead of
`virtualMachine.pause()` and `virtualMachine.resume()` respectively.

This meant pausing or resuming a VM would restart it instead.
Simplified both methods to use the async VZVirtualMachine APIs directly.

* fix(lume): avoid actor-isolation error in pause/resume

* docs(lume): sync generated reference docs

* ci(docs): configure lychee root dir for absolute links

* docs(cua): fix broken HUD environments link

commit.message:
fix(lume): use cached disk mode for Linux VMs to prevent filesystem corruption (#1100)

Apple's Virtualization framework has a known issue where the default
`.automatic` caching mode for VZDiskImageStorageDeviceAttachment causes
EXT4/filesystem corruption in Linux guest VMs. This has been independently
discovered and fixed by both UTM and Lima:

- UTM: https://github.com/utmapp/UTM/pull/5919
- Lima: https://github.com/lima-vm/lima/pull/2026

The fix sets `cachingMode: .cached` for virtio block devices in
LinuxVirtualizationService. macOS VMs are unaffected and continue to
use `.automatic`.

Symptoms without this fix include EXT4 checksum errors on boot,
read-only filesystem remounts, and data loss — particularly on VMs
stored on external drives.

commit.message:
feat(skills): rewrite cua-do-cli as task-oriented gui-automation skill (#1121)

Rename skills/cua-do-cli → skills/gui-automation and rewrite SKILL.md
from a tool-oriented reference ("here's a CLI called cua do") into a
task-oriented skill ("you can test GUIs, fill forms, verify layouts").

The old skill organized flows around technical context (API key set vs
not set). The new skill organizes around user tasks: test a button, fill
a form, handle file uploads, visual verification, E2E flows, drag-and-
drop, and fuzz testing. This makes the skill discoverable — when an agent
sees "gui-automation" and reads "test buttons, fill forms, verify visual
layouts," it knows to use this skill for GUI tasks.

Other changes:
- Extract full command reference to references/command-reference.md
- Add trajectory sharing as end-of-task behavior (always share link)
- Add snapshot vs screenshot as a guardrail note, not a separate flow
- Directory name matches frontmatter name per Agent Skills spec

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(lume): prevent ls hangs, add SSH fallback, fix bridged IP resolution

- NetworkUtils: add runWithTimeout helper that kills stuck subprocesses
  (lsof, nc, ping) after a deadline, preventing lume ls from hanging
  during concurrent VM state transitions

- SSH: add SystemSSHClient using /usr/bin/ssh with SSH_ASKPASS as
  fallback when NIO SSH cannot establish direct TCP connections (e.g.,
  in sandboxed environments where macOS blocks non-system-signed
  binaries from accessing vmnet interfaces)

- DHCPLeaseParser: add ARP table lookup for bridged mode IP resolution.
  In bridged mode, VMs get IPs from the physical network DHCP server
  (not vmnet), so the ARP table is the correct source. ARP entries are
  preferred over DHCP leases to avoid stale NAT IPs from previous
  sessions.

commit.message: Bump lume to v0.2.83
Bump lume to v0.2.82

feat(lume): restructure release as .app bundle with bridged networking (#1122)

* fix(lume): update test mocks to match current API

- Update MockVM.run() signature to include networkMode and clipboard
  parameters added to the VM base class
- Update VMDetailsPrinterTests to expect the network column in table
  output

* feat(lume): restructure release binary as .app bundle for bridged networking

Restructure the lume release artifact from a standalone CLI binary into a
macOS .app bundle so that a provisioning profile can be loaded by the OS,
enabling the com.apple.vm.networking restricted entitlement for bridged
networking support in release builds.

Closes #1076

* fix(lume): fetch notarization log on failure for debugging

* fix(lume): fix codesign for notarization - add timestamp, fix entitlements flag, show errors

* fix(lume): add codesign verification and use ditto for signature-safe copy

* fix(lume): add keychain to search list and pass --keychain to codesign

* fix(lume): sign resource bundle before binary (inside-out signing order)

* fix(lume): use --deep codesign, move resource bundle to Resources/

The lume_lume.bundle is a flat SPM resource directory (no Info.plist),
not a proper macOS bundle. codesign was failing with "bundle format
unrecognized" which caused silent fallback to adhoc signing.

Fix: use --deep on the .app bundle so codesign handles nested code
automatically and seals flat resource directories properly.

* fix(lume): remove resource bundle from Contents/MacOS to fix codesign

The lume_lume.bundle is a flat SPM resource directory without Info.plist.
When placed in Contents/MacOS/, codesign fails with "bundle format
unrecognized" and silently falls back to adhoc signing.

Move it to Contents/Resources/ only, which codesign seals as data.

* fix(lume): update install-local.sh and build-release.sh to match resource bundle fix

Move lume_lume.bundle to Contents/Resources/ instead of Contents/MacOS/
to avoid codesign "bundle format unrecognized" errors. Also fix
--entitlement -> --entitlements typo in build-release.sh.

* fix(lume): place SPM resource bundle at .app root for Bundle.module resolution

SPM's auto-generated Bundle.module looks up resources via
Bundle.main.bundleURL (the .app root), NOT Bundle.main.resourceURL
(Contents/Resources/). Placing lume_lume.bundle in Contents/Resources/
would cause a fatal crash at runtime when Bundle.module tries to load it.

Move the resource bundle to the .app root level across all three build
scripts (build-release-notarized.sh, build-release.sh, install-local.sh).
This keeps it out of Contents/MacOS/ (which breaks codesign) while
ensuring SPM can find it at runtime.

Also adds *.provisionprofile to .gitignore.

* fix(lume): fix Bundle.module resolution for .app bundle resource loading

SPM's auto-generated Bundle.module looks up resources via
Bundle.main.bundleURL (the .app root), but codesign rejects content
at the .app root ("unsealed contents") and in Contents/MacOS/
("bundle format unrecognized"). The only valid location for codesign
is Contents/Resources/, but Bundle.module doesn't check there.

Solution:
- Add Bundle.lumeResources custom accessor that checks resourceURL
  first (for .app bundles) then bundleURL (for standalone binaries)
- Replace all Bundle.module usages in UnattendedConfig.swift
- Revert build scripts to place lume_lume.bundle in Contents/Resources/

The unused SPM-generated Bundle.module is never accessed, so its
fatalError path is never triggered.

commit.message:
fix(agent): add defensive coordinate scaling for high-res VMs (#1108)

* fix(agent): add defensive coordinate scaling for high-res VMs

Anthropic's computer-use API internally downscales screenshots larger
than ~XGA, causing coordinate mismatches when the VM runs at higher
resolutions. Add defensive scaling in the agent SDK: downscale
screenshots before sending to the API, cap declared display dimensions,
and upscale returned coordinates. For 1024x768 VMs scale factors are
1.0 so there is no behavioral change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* style: format local_image.py with black

Pre-existing formatting issue caught by CI.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: regenerate Python SDK documentation

Auto-generated docs out of sync with source code. Updates version
numbers and reflects upstream Sentry removal.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(agent,computer): add debug logging for coordinate and resolution scaling

Adds debug-level logging to all scaling operations so we can trace when
and where resolution/coordinate transforms occur without surfacing noise
to users unless they opt into verbose logging.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(agent): reset scale_factors per screenshot to prevent stale upscaling

scale_factors was only initialized once before the message loop, so a
downscaled screenshot would leave stale upscale factors that incorrectly
applied to subsequent screenshots that didn't need downscaling. Reset to
(1.0, 1.0) at the start of each computer_call_output with an input_image.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Add interactive terminal (PTY) support w/ tests to cua-auto, computer-server, computer, and the cua CLI (#1114)

Every cua do action is now automatically recorded to a replayable

commit.message: Bump @trycua/playground to v0.2.2

fix(playground): fetch trajectory screenshots client-side to avoid 504 (#1096)

* fix(playground): fix computer selection and make baseUrl optional

- Add computer property to draftChat in Playground.tsx to fix
  "Please select a computer" error when sending messages from empty state
- Prefer running computers, fall back to first available
- Make baseUrl optional in InferenceConfig type since computer server
  has a default inference endpoint (https://inference.cua.ai/v1)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(playground): fetch trajectory screenshots client-side to avoid 504

Replace ZIP-based trajectory replay with client-side screenshot fetching.
The server now returns lightweight JSON (signed URLs + agent responses),
and the client fetches screenshots in parallel directly from Supabase CDN.
This eliminates the serverless function timeout on large chats.

commit.message:
Add documentation link checker CI workflow (#1034)

* Fix CuaBench docs 404 by correcting broken example links

The examples section is a sibling of guide, not nested under it.
All links using /cuabench/guide/examples/ were incorrect and should
use /cuabench/examples/ instead. Fixed in navigation header and
4 MDX content files.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Q3U3p5HjFJfQRjicuhPEpW

* Add documentation link checker for internal and external links

Adds a TypeScript script that scans all MDX and TSX files for broken
links. Validates internal links against the docs page tree and
optionally checks external URLs with HTTP requests.

- `pnpm docs:check-links` for internal links only
- `pnpm docs:check-links:external` for internal + external
- CI workflow triggers on docs content/src changes
- Skips static assets (images, fonts, etc.)

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Q3U3p5HjFJfQRjicuhPEpW

* Replace custom link checker with next-validate-link + lychee

Use next-validate-link (by the Fumadocs author) for internal link
validation with proper MDX parsing via remark. Use lychee GitHub
Action for external link checking in CI.

- next-validate-link: validates internal cross-references in MDX
- lychee: fast Rust-based external URL checker with caching
- CI runs both checks in parallel on docs changes

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Q3U3p5HjFJfQRjicuhPEpW

* Fix 15 broken documentation links across 8 files (#1045)

- Remove trailing slashes from /cua/guide/fundamentals/vlms/ links
- Remove incorrect /docs prefix from internal links in vnc-recorder and
  demonstration-guided-skills
- Fix cli-playbook → cloud-cli path in cloud-cli reference
- Replace /lume with /lume/guide/getting-started/introduction (no index page)
- Fix /lume/guide/advanced/unattended-setup → /lume/guide/fundamentals/unattended-setup
- Remove links from Linux Container and QEMU Container headings (no index pages)

https://claude.ai/code/session_01AsxA3MLUo6xMgFUD5e43Dv

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remove deprecated --exclude-mail flag from lychee action (#1046)

Lychee v0.21.0 removed the --exclude-mail flag. Mail addresses are now
excluded by default, so the flag is no longer needed.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01SGxvjUaBKjgwQuLyDQz1HS

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: update broken domain links (cua.dev -> cua.ai, openclaw.dev -> openclaw.ai) (#1050)

https://claude.ai/code/session_01UUas213X98Fr6xKRarmvRu

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: resolve broken documentation links across docs (#1051)

- Create index pages for linux-container and qemu-container directories
  to fix internal link checker errors
- Replace broken external cua.ai/docs/cuabot/* links with internal paths
  in cuabot changelog
- Replace broken docs-woad-phi.vercel.app migration guide link with
  inline heading in agent-sdk changelog
- Convert external cua.ai/docs/get-started/quickstart to internal link
  in post-event-contact-export example
- Remove dead openclaw.ai/docs/vps-hosting link from openclaw example

https://claude.ai/code/session_012v9UqLpLqpjoJy7XXnjwL2

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Exclude file:// URLs from lychee docs link checker (#1056)

The lychee external link checker was resolving relative markdown
links (e.g., ./screenspot-v2, ../hud) as file:// URLs and failing
because the target files use .mdx extensions not present in the links.
Internal/relative links are already validated by the next-validate-link
job, so lychee should only check external HTTP(S) links.

This matches the approach already used in ci-check-links.yml.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01TCohF6h4raHA1o4eQjhsdP

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

readme_html:
Choose Your Path

CuaBot - Co-op computer-use for any agent

cuabot gives any coding agent a seamless sandbox for computer-use. Individual windows appear natively on your desktop with H.265, shared clipboard, and audio.

npx cuabot                 # Setup onboarding

# Run any agent in a sandbox
cuabot claude              # Claude Code
cuabot openclaw            # OpenClaw in the sandbox

# Run any GUI workflow in a sandbox
cuabot chromium
cuabot --screenshot
cuabot --type "hello"
cuabot --click <x> <y> [button]

Built-in support for agent-browser and agent-device (iOS, Android) out of the box.

Get Started | Installation | First spotted at ClawCon

Cua - Agentic UI Automation & Code Execution

Build agents that see screens, click buttons, and complete tasks autonomously. Run isolated code execution environments for AI coding assistants like Claude Code, Codex CLI, or OpenCode.

# Requires Python 3.12 or 3.13
from computer import Computer
from agent import ComputerAgent

computer = Computer(os_type="linux", provider_type="cloud")
agent = ComputerAgent(model="anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929", computer=computer)

async for result in agent.run([{"role": "user", "content": "Open Firefox and search for Cua"}]):
    print(result)

Get Started | Examples | API Reference

Cua-Bench - Benchmarks & RL Environments

Evaluate computer-use agents on OSWorld, ScreenSpot, Windows Arena, and custom tasks. Export trajectories for training.

# Install and create base image
cd cua-bench
uv tool install -e . && cb image create linux-docker

# Run benchmark with agent
cb run dataset datasets/cua-bench-basic --agent cua-agent --max-parallel 4

Get Started | Partner With Us | Registry | CLI Reference

Lume - macOS Virtualization

Create and manage macOS/Linux VMs with near-native performance on Apple Silicon using Apple's Virtualization.Framework.

# Install Lume
/bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/trycua/cua/main/libs/lume/scripts/install.sh)"

# Pull & start a macOS VM
lume run macos-sequoia-vanilla:latest

Get Started | FAQ | CLI Reference

Packages

Package Description
cuabot Multi-agent computer-use sandbox CLI
cua-agent AI agent framework for computer-use tasks
cua-computer SDK for controlling desktop environments
cua-computer-server Driver for UI interactions and code execution in sandboxes
cua-bench Benchmarks and RL environments for computer-use
lume macOS/Linux VM management on Apple Silicon
lumier Docker-compatible interface for Lume VMs
